architecture.summary: Universal Sparse Autoencoders (USAEs) learn a shared, overcomplete sparse dictionary that encodes activations from multiple pretrained vision models into a unified concept space and decodes them back to reconstruct each model's activations. During training, a single model is randomly selected per iteration; its activations are encoded into the shared sparse code via a model-specific encoder, then decoded via all model-specific decoders to reconstruct every model's activations. Backpropagation updates only the chosen encoder-decoder pair against the aggregated reconstruction loss across all models, enforcing concept alignment during end-to-end training.

biblio.title: Universal Sparse Autoencoders: Interpretable Cross-Model Concept Alignment

claims.statement: USAEs discover semantically coherent universal concepts across vision models ranging from low-level features (e.g., colors and textures) to higher-level structures (e.g., parts and objects)
Self-reconstruction R² scores for DinoV2 average 0.8, compared to 0.7 for SigLIP and ViT on final layer activations

Over 1000 concepts exhibit peak firing entropy, confirming the USAE learns a strongly universal concept space; bimodal distribution shows a second group with moderate entropy indicating concepts that favor two models but not all three
The first 200 concepts co-fire between 50–80% of the time across all three models, indicating a core set of consistently recognized concepts

When focusing on high-frequency co-firing concepts, the correlation between concept co-firing frequency and concept energy strengthens from r=0.35 (all concepts) to r=0.66 (frequently co-firing concepts), suggesting a strong correlation between how energetic a concept is and its universality

ViT has the strongest concept overlap with 41% of its universal concepts having a cosine similarity ≥0.5 with its independent SAE counterpart; SigLIP and DinoV2 achieve 30% and 36% respectively; USAEs achieve AUC=0.30 versus baseline AUC=0.13 on concept consistency matching

DinoV2 exhibits unique geometric and perspective-related concepts including converging perspective lines, view-invariant angle/tilt features, and low-level 3D understanding concepts such as rectangular prism faces and brim lines of dome-shaped objects, which are absent or low-entropy in SigLIP and ViT

Only a relatively low proportion of concepts show high overlap between universal and independently trained SAEs (30% for SigLIP, 36% for DinoV2 at cosine similarity ≥0.5), indicating universal training discovers concepts that may not emerge in independent training
